scheduling-kit is the reusable scheduling library repo.

Purpose:
- provide backend-agnostic scheduling contracts
- provide payment adapters
- provide Svelte checkout components
- provide reusable Effect-powered orchestration
- support migrations away from closed scheduling vendors without hard-coding a single app

Boundary:
- this repo is not the place for Playwright browser automation or Modal deployment logic
- browser automation belongs in `@tummycrypt/scheduling-bridge`
- site-specific admin UI belongs in the application repo

Build truth:
- pnpm is the real packaging and publish path
- Bazel is the hermetic conformity/build graph, not yet the direct publish path
- keep `package.json`, `MODULE.bazel`, and `BUILD.bazel` aligned

CI/publish:
- CI runs on Node 20 and 22
- important commands:
  - pnpm check
  - pnpm lint
  - pnpm test:unit
  - pnpm test:integration
  - pnpm build
  - pnpm exec publint
- publish targets:
  - npm: @tummycrypt/scheduling-kit
  - GitHub Packages: @jesssullivan/scheduling-kit

Current tracking:
- milestone: Sprint T: Migration Platform Contracts
- issue: #41 downstream migration/onboarding contract

Architecture rails:
- keep library contracts reusable across multiple businesses
- use Effect where it clarifies workflow/resource/error boundaries
- do not cargo-cult Effect into every simple helper
- keep MassageIthaca-specific policy out of core abstractions

Files to inspect first:
- package.json
- MODULE.bazel
- BUILD.bazel
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/publish.yml
- src/core/**
- src/adapters/**
- src/payments/**
- src/testing/**
